Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a pseudo BPMN representation of a process Lets break it down step by step and explain each part

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The process begins with a Start Event Then we have a Task A Receive Customer Request After that there is an exclusive XOR Gateway that checks the Request Type There are two types Standard and Custom

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For Standard Path Task B1 Perform Standard Validation Then a parallel gateway AND splits the flow into two tasks that run in parallel Task C1 Credit Check Task C2 Inventory Check After both parallel tasks are completed they join the AND gateway join and then proceed to Task D Calculate Delivery Date

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For Custom Path Task B2 Perform Custom Feasibility Analysis Then an XOR Gateway Is Customization Feasible If Yes Task E1 Prepare Custom Quotation If No Task E2 Send Rejection Notice End Event So if not feasible the process ends

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After the standard or custom path tasks are completed that is after Task D for standard or after Task E1 for custom but note for custom if it goes to E1 then it continues if it goes to E2 the process ends the process then moves to another part

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The pseudo BPMN says After Standard or Custom Path Tasks Completed and then we have another XOR Gateway Is Approval Needed

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Approval Gateway If approval is needed Task F Obtain Manager Approval Then another XOR Gateway Is Approval Granted If Yes Task G Generate Final Invoice If No Task H Re evaluate Conditions then loop back to Task E1 for Custom Path or Task D for Standard Path If no approval needed Task G Generate Final Invoice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then after Task G the invoice generation we have Task I Send Confirmation to Customer and then End Event

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: This means that if the request was standard we go back to Task D Calculate Delivery Date and then presumably re run the approval process Similarly for custom we go back to Task E1 Prepare Custom Quotation and then re run the approval

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Standard Path A to B1 to C1 and C2 in parallel to join to D to then check approval if needed and granted then G to I to End If approval not granted then H to loop back to D

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Custom Path feasible A to B2 to feasibility gateway to if yes E1 to then check approval if needed and granted G to I to End If approval not granted then H to loop back to E1

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Custom Path not feasible A to B2 to feasibility gateway to if no E2 to End Event

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now lets note that the process after the custom standard paths converges at the approval gateway However note that the loop back goes to two different tasks E1 or D Also note that if the process ends at E2 we dont go to the approval part

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Task A Receive Customer Request The process is triggered by receiving a customer request This could be an order or an inquiry

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Task B1 Perform Standard Validation For standard requests validate the request for example check if the requested items are available check customer details and so on

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Parallel Tasks C1 and C2 For standard requests perform two checks in parallel to save time Credit Check Verify the customers creditworthiness Inventory Check Check if the items are in stock and available for delivery

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Task D Calculate Delivery Date For standard requests after passing the parallel checks calculate when the order can be delivered

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Task B2 Perform Custom Feasibility Analysis For custom requests analyze whether the customization can be done for example technical feasibility resource availability cost

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Gateway Is Customization Feasible For custom requests determine if the customization is feasible If Yes proceed to prepare a custom quotation If No send a rejection notice and end the process

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Task E1 Prepare Custom Quotation Create a detailed quotation for the custom request including price timeline and any special conditions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Task E2 Send Rejection Notice Inform the customer that the custom request cannot be fulfilled

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Gateway Is Approval Needed After either the standard path Task D or the custom path Task E1 determine if the order requires managerial approval for example due to value risk or exception handling

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Task F Obtain Manager Approval Submit the order to a manager for approval This might involve presenting the details and waiting for a decision

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Task H Re evaluate Conditions This means that the manager has rejected the order so the process needs to adjust for example change price terms or even reject the order Then the process loops back to either Task E1 for custom or Task D for standard to adjust the quotation or delivery date and then re apply for approval

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Task G Generate Final Invoice Create the official invoice for the customer Task I Send Confirmation to Customer Send the order confirmation and invoice to the customer End Event The process ends

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Convergence and Looping The standard and custom paths converge at the approval gateway the one that checks if approval is needed However the custom path that ends with E2 does not converge here because the process ends there The loop back occurs when approval is not granted The process then goes back to either Task E1 custom or Task D standard to rework the order and then re enter the approval process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Differences between Standard and Custom Paths Standard path uses a standardized validation and parallel checks credit and inventory and then a straightforward delivery date calculation Custom path requires a feasibility analysis and if feasible a custom quotation is prepared The custom path might involve more complex pricing and planning

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Conditions for Approval or Rework Approval is needed based on some criteria not specified in the process after the initial validation and quotation steps This could be based on order value customer risk or other business rules

Name: Validation - PE
Text: If approval is not granted then the conditions are re evaluated Task H and the process loops back to adjust the order either the delivery date for standard or the quotation for custom

Name: Validation - IND
Text: The process does not specify an exit condition for the loop like maximum attempts In practice there would be a limit or an alternative ending if the adjustments cannot satisfy the manager
